title: Build "Correction Capture → Enforced Constraint" (Never Twice) as the next killer feature
context: A 34-agent market-research swarm (Reddit, YouTube, 13 competitors, HN/X, Claude Code issues) ranked 13 demand clusters and adversarially verified 6 candidate features across 3 lenses (demand-real, differentiation, feasibility). This was the only candidate to pass all 3 lenses cleanly (3/3). The pain — 'feedback gets stored then ignored' (the Alzheimer problem) — is dollar-quantified: Claude Code issue #40801 ($600-$850 overrun, closed 'not planned') and the arXiv 'Trace' paper (memory alone leaves 57.5% of preference checks violated).
decision: Ship hunch_record_correction: when a human corrects the agent, mint a first-class scoped Constraint with provenance=human_confirmed, then enforce it via the existing pre-edit hook DENY + CI Constraint Guard. MVP = new MCP write tool (mirrors hunch_record_decision/record-constraint, builds a Constraint rec) + a correction-cue nudge in the UserPromptSubmit hook branch; reuse blockingInScope() and the CI guard unchanged. The moat is the full arc (auto-mint-from-correction → scoped enforced invariant → portable across all assistants via one git-native .hunch graph), not the deny primitive itself.
consequences: Differentiation must be framed as the cross-assistant, git-native, auto-minted-from-correction packaging — never 'the only tool that enforces' (Cursor v1.7 hooks + CodeRabbit already block).
Default firmness is advisory: edit-time DENY is opt-in (strict); CI blocking works by default. Don't oversell edit-time blocking.
Scope inference must default conservative (component glob, not **) to avoid a blocking correction muting all edits under strict.
alternatives_rejected: Why-Capture at commit time — failed feasibility (post-commit hook is detached, synthesis takes seconds; a sub-second commit interview collapses to always-skip)
Regression Tripwires — failed demand-real (anecdote was hypothetical) and hook lacks line numbers
Executable Invariants / Invariant Proof Gate — failed differentiation (Agent RuleZ and CodeRabbit Pre-Merge already ship near-identical)
